title: fraxinus excelsior f. verrucosa
intro: Fraxinus excelsior f. verrucosa, also known as the aspen or quaking aspen, is a variant of the European ash tree (Fraxinus excelsior) characterized by its distinctive white, warty bark. This tree has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues. The health benefits of Fraxinus excelsior f. verrucosa include its ability to act as an expectorant, reducing mucus production and relieving congestion, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Fraxinus excelsior f. verrucosa can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
